arguments.query: Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: OD=1600 mm, P=1.55 MPa, T=350°C, material SM-105, Type 5 joint with Spot RT, CA=2.0 mm.
Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: OD=1600 mm, P=1.55 MPa, T=350°C, material SM-105, Type 5 joint with Spot RT, CA=2.0 mm.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

arguments.query: Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: ID=950 mm, P=2.6 MPa, T=325°C, material SM-106 Gr B, Type 1 joint with Full RT, CA=1.0 mm.
Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: ID=950 mm, P=2.6 MPa, T=325°C, material SM-106 Gr B, Type 1 joint with Full RT, CA=1.0 mm.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) SM-516 Gr 70 shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C.

(b) SM-106 Gr B shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C and is not permitted for cyclic service above 400°C without additional analysis.

---

query: Where does SPES-1 define corrosion allowance?

arguments.query: corrosion allowance definition
corrosion allowance definition

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

query: What does A-25 require designers to add to calculated thickness?

arguments.query: A-25 requirements for calculated thickness
A-25 requirements for calculated thickness

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

query: How do A-27 and Table B-12 interact in a shell thickness calculation?

arguments.query: A-27 and Table B-12 shell thickness calculation
A-27 and Table B-12 shell thickness calculation

chunks.content:
(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

arguments.query: A-27 and Table M-1 interaction for allowable stress
A-27 and Table M-1 interaction for allowable stress

chunks.content:
(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

query: Which source gives the corrosion allowance rule and how is it combined with A-27 thickness?

arguments.query: corrosion allowance rule
corrosion allowance rule

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

arguments.query: corrosion allowance combination with A-27 thickness
corrosion allowance combination with A-27 thickness

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

query: Which SPES-1 provisions support MAWP after selecting nominal thickness?

arguments.query: MAWP after selecting nominal thickness
MAWP after selecting nominal thickness

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

SPES-1 paragraphs for Table M-1 allowable stresses and M-23 temperature limits for stainless materials

chunks.content:
(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) SM-516 Gr 70 shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C.

(b) SM-106 Gr B shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C and is not permitted for cyclic service above 400°C without additional analysis.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |

```

```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

|---|---|---|---|---|

| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

arguments.query: UG-27 pressure vessel calculation
UG-27 pressure vessel calculation

chunks.content:
(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

|---|---|---|---|---|

| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |

| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

arguments.query: simple formula pressure applicability limit
simple formula pressure applicability limit

chunks.content:
(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

arguments.query: nominal plate selection relationship with corrosion allowance and calculated thickness in SPES-1
nominal plate selection relationship with corrosion allowance and calculated thickness in SPES-1

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

arguments.query: ASME Section VIII Division 2 fatigue screening requirements
ASME Section VIII Division 2 fatigue screening requirements

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

```

```

```

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

arguments.query: required inputs for pressure vessel thickness calculation
required inputs for pressure vessel thickness calculation

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.
